content.text: Roles define what users can see and do within the cms dashboard

It is important to note that Alinea automatically creates a default Admin role for every project. This role is granted full access to every workspace, root, and entry. You do not need to define this role manually, and it ensures that there is always at least one user type with unrestricted management capabilities.

Within the permissions function, you define the access policy. You can use allow to grant access and deny to strictly forbid it. By default, permissions are hierarchical, but you can use grant: 'explicit' to prevent rights from automatically trickling down to child entries.
In Alinea currently once you deny something you cant allow it again down the stream, as the deny cant be overwritten so be careful when doing this
